name: White Rice

disambiguationNotes: Do not substitute cooked rice values.
This file is explicitly locked to the located enriched SR Legacy row; do not drift back to unenriched values without changing the identity lock and source row together.
Do not substitute parboiled, instant, jasmine, basmati, or short-grain values unless the identity lock is intentionally changed.

secondarySources.note: Exact row identified in this pass. This is the current primary source anchor for the canonical uncooked white-rice entry and should remain explicit about being enriched rather than silently mixed with unenriched values.

changeNotes: Production draft upgraded with explicit USDA anchor fdcId 168877 / ndbNumber 20044.
Canonical FoodRanked white rice is now locked to uncooked long-grain white rice using this enriched raw row as the current primary source anchor.
The file no longer treats enriched-vs-unenriched as unresolved debate inside the JSON; any future change should be an intentional source/identity replacement, not drift.

kcal.sourceName: USDA FoodData Central API food details
kcal.note: Canonical anchor row for energy on the locked enriched rice record. Current file value 365 matches the located row.

protein_g.sourceName: USDA FoodData Central API food details
protein_g.note: Canonical anchor row for protein. Current file value 7.1 is aligned with the located row value 7.13.

carb_g.sourceName: USDA FoodData Central API food details
carb_g.note: Canonical anchor row for carbohydrate by difference. Current file value 80 is aligned with the located row value 79.95.

omega3_mg.sourceName: USDA FoodData Central API food details
omega3_mg.note: Adjusted to a conservative ~31 mg total omega-3-equivalent from the attached enriched white-rice fatty-acid rows.

cholesterol_mg.sourceName: USDA FoodData Central API food details
cholesterol_mg.note: Plant-food cholesterol is explicitly 0 mg on the attached USDA row, so this should be 0 rather than null.

essential_amino_acids_score.note: Displayed as N/A for now. No defensible reusable amino-acid scoring method has been attached for this exact food identity.

nonessential_amino_acids_score.note: Displayed as N/A for now. No defensible reusable amino-acid scoring method has been attached for this exact food identity.

glycemic_index.note: GI is still unresolved because the attached USDA row is raw enriched rice and GI depends strongly on variety and preparation.

bioavailability_percent.note: Displayed as N/A for now. This metric remains part of the final display schema, but the project does not yet have a defensible sourced method for this white-rice identity.

pros.title: easy to digest for many people
pros.explanation: This is one of the clearest real-world use cases for white rice and is more defensible than vague staple praise.

evidence.citationNote: Common practical observation, but too broad and context-dependent to present as hard scoring evidence.

pros.title: can fit low-fibre or bland-food situations
pros.explanation: This is a situational upside, not a signal that the grain is nutritionally elite.

pros.title: extremely practical staple base
pros.explanation: Practicality matters for food choice, but this should not be allowed to overpower the refinement penalty in nutrition ranking.

evidence.citationNote: Narration context rather than strong nutritional evidence.

cons.title: fibre is weak for a grain
cons.explanation: Low fibre is one of the most defensible reasons white rice falls behind better whole-grain peers.

evidence.citationNote: This claim is directly supported by the nutrient profile, assuming the source row is locked correctly.

cons.title: glycemic behaviour is rougher than stronger grains
cons.explanation: This is a fair directional critique, but the exact severity depends heavily on variety and preparation.

evidence.citationNote: Use only after an exact GI citation is attached for this locked rice form.

cons.title: refinement strips away much of the grain's upside
cons.explanation: This is the core nutrition argument against treating white rice as interchangeable with intact grains.

evidence.sourceName: grain refinement literature; exact citation still to be attached
evidence.citationNote: Strongly supported in general, but the exact citation bundle is still missing from the file.

primaryRemainingBlockers: Attach a form-specific GI citation for uncooked long-grain white rice or document the display value as explicit N/A if not defensibly sourceable for this exact identity.
Keep enriched status explicit in all future white-rice data handling unless the canonical source row is intentionally replaced.
Treat other white-rice forms such as cooked, parboiled, basmati, jasmine, or unenriched as separate future entries rather than variants of this one.

sourceNotes: Draft production-lane entry now carries explicit USDA anchor fdcId 168877 / ndbNumber 20044.
Canonical FoodRanked white rice is now locked to uncooked long-grain white rice using this enriched raw row as the current primary source anchor.
GI remains unresolved because the attached USDA row is raw enriched rice while GI depends strongly on variety and preparation.
